session.session_title: PI-beta build-closure — de-file + kill snapshots (Production Multi-Tenant API)
session.session_executive_summary: PI-beta (de-file + kill-snapshots, PRJ-019) built + closed on pi-beta-defile, merged to main. Finishes the runtime's exit from the per-engagement-file + JSON-snapshot world. Now one unified DB; the active engagement is named per request by the X-Engagement header (no marker). De-filed: registry served from the unified engagements table (meta DB deleted); marker, activation-worker, dogfood/lazy migrators, marker_guard, and the /admin reroute removed; switching is a client-side context change that removes the 'Switching failed at step 3' bug. Killed snapshots: exporter, catalog exporter, export-dir gate, force_export, Settings.export_dir, the db-export/ tree, and the file-watch RefreshService. Docs rewritten to DB-as-source-of-truth. SQLite suite green. Records DEC-378 and resolves PI-126.
session.session_description: PI-beta build-closure: ingests the four branch commits (Architecture/scoping -> slices 1-3 de-file -> slice 4 kill-snapshots -> slices 5-6 doc rewrites), records the build as DEC-378, and resolves PI-126. PI-beta was built directly (no ADO phase decomposition), so there is no workstream-completion step. Merged FF to main at eac4b016.

conversation.conversation_purpose: Close out PI-beta: ingest the build commits, record the build decision, and resolve PI-126.
conversation.conversation_description: Built the de-file + kill-snapshots work (registry on unified DB, header-per-request engagement, deleted meta/marker/activation/snapshot machinery, doc rewrites) and authored this build-closure.
conversation.conversation_summary: Resolved PI-126; recorded DEC-378; ingested the four PI-beta commits.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-beta Architecture/scoping pass — de-file + kill snapshots + API/MCP-only
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-beta Architecture/scoping pass — de-file + kill snapshots + API/MCP-only

PI-beta's Architecture phase (refines program design D5-partial/D6/D7/D8) for
PRJ-019. Build-ready removal plan grounded in a full dependency survey of the
meta-DB layer, the snapshot/export machinery, the per-engagement-file routing +
activation worker, and the orientation file-fallback.

Decisions: /engagements serves the unified engagements table (meta DB deleted);
engagement is per-request via X-Engagement with the current_engagement.json marker
removed (switching = client-side context change -> deletes ui/activation_worker
and the broken "Switching failed at step 3"); session_scope collapses to
flush->commit and db-export/ is deleted (git trail = close-out payloads + deposit
logs); one-shot migrators retired; orientation becomes MCP/REST-only. Six-slice
removal order each leaving the suite green. Flags the PI-alpha sequencing: the two
share db.py/engagement_routing/conftest edits, so land PI-alpha first and build
PI-beta off the post-PI-alpha main.

Branch pi-beta-defile off main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-beta slices 1-3 — registry on unified DB, delete meta layer, de-file runtime (SUITE GREEN)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-beta slices 1-3 — registry on unified DB, delete meta layer, de-file runtime (SUITE GREEN)

Folds PI-beta Development slices 1+2+3 into one green commit (squashes the
prior WIP 6d58c5c). The runtime no longer pretends there are many
per-engagement databases: there is one unified DB, the engagement is named
per request by the X-Engagement header, and switching engagements is a
client-side context change.

Slice 1 — /engagements on the unified DB:
- access/engagement.py operates on the unified `engagements` table via a
  normal session (no meta-snapshot hook); api/routers/engagements.py uses
  readonly/writable_session, not meta_session.

Slice 2 — delete the meta DB layer:
- removed access/meta_db.py, meta_models.py, meta_exporter.py,
  migration/meta_alembic.py, migrations/meta/; dropped api/deps.meta_session
  and the api/main meta bootstrap+migration; version_info reports the unified
  DB's single schema; /admin drops the meta schema + the in-process reroute.

Slice 3 — de-file the runtime (header-only engagement resolution):
- deleted ui/activation_worker.py, migration/dogfood_v0_5.py, api/marker_guard.py,
  migration/lazy_migration.py; gutted runtime/engagement_routing.py to the
  export-write gate only; engagement_scoping_enabled defaults True; scope
  middleware resolves the engagement from X-Engagement alone (no marker fallback).
- StorageClient sends X-Engagement from the active-engagement context;
  ActiveEngagementContext is in-memory (no current_engagement.json marker);
  MainWindow.switch_engagement performs a client-side context switch — which
  removes the broken "Switching failed at step 3" subprocess swap by construction;
  new_engagement_dialog is create-and-select; cli.run_api + ui/app startup
  simplified; managers plumbing removed.

Tests: deleted the per-file/activation/marker/dogfood/two-DB/launcher/v0.5-e2e
suites; repointed the engagement-registry, scope-middleware, routing, and
active-context tests at the unified DB; API-backed test clients (UI panels,
MCP, apply_close_out, /engagements) now send X-Engagement since the TestClient
runs the app in a portal thread that does not inherit the seeded scope ContextVar.

Slice 4 (kill db-export/snapshots) and slices 5-6 (doc rewrites) remain separate.

SQLite suite green: 2246 passed; the only failures are pre-existing and
environmental — apply_close_out's 17 main-branch-guard refusals (expected
off main) and test_0037's alembic-chain-from-scratch NoSuchTableError:charter
(the gitignored base-entity-catalog YAMLs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-beta slice 4 — kill the JSON-snapshot/db-export machinery (SUITE GREEN)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-beta slice 4 — kill the JSON-snapshot/db-export machinery (SUITE GREEN)

Removes the snapshot/export write-path (D-β3). The database is now the sole
source of truth; clients read it via the API/MCP, and the git-tracked audit
trail is the close-out payloads + deposit-event logs (kept), not regenerated
JSON snapshots.

Write-path core:
- deleted access/exporter.py; session_scope() drops the export hook + the
  export= parameter (now flush→commit only); force_export removed.
- deleted runtime/engagement_routing.py (only the export gate remained after
  slice 3); removed assert_export_dir_ready + UNCONFIGURED_SENTINEL and the
  EngagementExportDir* exceptions (runtime/exceptions.py); removed the
  Settings.export_dir field + its default/validator.
- api/deps writable/readonly_session drop export=; api/errors + api/main drop
  the engagement_export_dir handler.

Catalog + apply + tree:
- deleted access/repositories/catalog/exports.py (the per-entity catalog JSON
  exporter) and its 8 write-path calls + re-exports; migration 0004 no longer
  imports the deleted suppression(); catalog_loader's suppress_exports is a
  no-op.
- apply_close_out.py no longer regenerates snapshots (the removed API hook
  did); branch-guard help text de-references db-export.
- deleted the git-tracked PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/db-export/ tree (73 files);
  the pre-commit hook now guards only deposit-event-logs/ (the surviving
  Model-A trail).

UI:
- deleted ui/refresh.py (the file-watch RefreshService watched the now-deleted
  snapshot tree); unwired it from MainWindow (snapshot_dir is an accepted-but-
  ignored arg) and the chat panel; removed the About dialog's Snapshot
  directory row. Panels refresh on selection, on lifecycle-ready, and via the
  manual Refresh button. The engagement_export_dir column + its CRUD field stay
  (no schema change this slice).

Tests: deleted the exporter / export-gate / refresh / catalog-export / chat-
PI106 / export-dir-handler suites; dropped export= from session_scope test
calls; removed snapshot-assertion tests (status/charter/migrate) and the
file-watch map/refresh panel tests; conftest v2_env no longer sets
CRMBUILDER_V2_EXPORT_DIR or calls force_export.

SQLite suite green: 2185 passed; the only failures remain pre-existing and
environmental — apply_close_out's 17 main-branch-guard refusals (off main) and
test_0037's alembic-chain-from-scratch NoSuchTableError:charter.

Deferred (separate): slices 5-6 doc rewrites (CLAUDE.md Tier-2 file-fallback,
governance-recording-rules, v2-user-process-guide); full MCP per-engagement
selection; the now-vestigial engagement_export_dir column + its UI (awaits a
schema-change pass).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-beta slices 5-6 — doc rewrites: orientation/recording-rules to DB-as-source-of-truth (D-β5)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-beta slices 5-6 — doc rewrites: orientation/recording-rules to DB-as-source-of-truth (D-β5)

Brings the operative docs in line with the de-filed, snapshot-free runtime (the
last in-scope PI-β Development work). No code change.

- CLAUDE.md: the Tier-2 orientation protocol is now "MCP or REST API, the two
  equal options" — read the DB live, never from committed files (the
  file-fallback bullet is gone). The session-lifecycle close-out no longer
  regenerates/commits db-export snapshots (commits the deposit-event log +
  deliverables). The branch-work protocol and "v0.1 landed" note drop db-export;
  the pre-commit hook now guards only deposit-event-logs/. Added a PI-β history
  note that supersedes the multi-engagement-file / marker / meta-DB /
  activation-worker / db-export descriptions where they conflict.
- governance-recording-rules.md: the apply-prompt "snapshot regeneration" step
  becomes "commit the deposit-event log + deliverables"; the "snapshot lag"
  failure mode becomes "reading committed files instead of the live DB."
- v2-user-process-guide.md: "file-fallback mode" → "REST API mode" throughout
  (operating-modes table, Tier-2 read, closeout database archive, the
  read-without-MCP path list); read the live API with the X-Engagement header.

Completes PI-β's Development + Documentation phases. Remaining: merge to main +
build-closure (records PI-β governance, resolves PI-126); deferred follow-ons
(MCP per-engagement selection, the vestigial engagement_export_dir column,
enumerate_commits repoint).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: PI-beta de-file + kill-snapshots — build decisions and findings
decisions.context: PRJ-019's Production Multi-Tenant API program decomposes into PI-alpha (Postgres foundation, Resolved), PI-beta (de-file + kill snapshots, PI-126), and PI-gamma (RBAC, PI-127). PI-123 had already made the data a single unified row-scoped DB; PI-beta removes the apparatus that still pretended there were many per-engagement files, plus the JSON-snapshot export process.
decisions.decision: (1) The engagement registry is served from the unified DB's engagements table; the separate meta DB (models, Alembic chain, meta_session) is deleted. (2) The active engagement is named per request by the X-Engagement header, resolved by scope_middleware; the current_engagement.json marker + fallback are removed and engagement_scoping_enabled defaults True. (3) Switching is a client-side context change (StorageClient header + MainWindow.switch_engagement), so ui/activation_worker, marker_guard, dogfood_v0_5, lazy_migration, and the /admin/active-engagement reroute are deleted — removing the 'Switching failed at step 3' bug by construction. (4) The JSON-snapshot machinery is removed: exporter.py, the catalog exporter, the export-dir gate, force_export, Settings.export_dir, the EngagementExportDir* exceptions, the git-tracked db-export/ tree, and the file-watch RefreshService; session_scope() is flush->commit only. (5) Docs (CLAUDE.md, governance-recording-rules, v2-user-process-guide) rewritten to DB-as-source-of-truth: orientation is MCP or REST, never committed files.
decisions.rationale: PI-123 made the per-engagement files data-unnecessary; the meta DB, marker, activation-worker, and snapshot exporter were dead apparatus that also carried a live bug. Header-per-request scoping matches the multi-tenant target (PI-gamma constrains the header to the principal later) and makes switching a cheap context change. Removing db-export eliminates a perpetually-lagging committed copy of state; the deposit-event logs + close-out payloads are the git-tracked governance trail.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Keep the marker as a default-engagement fallback (rejected: reintroduces the single-active process model the header replaces). Keep db-export as a read cache (rejected: it always lagged the live API and nothing should read committed state). Drop the engagement_export_dir column now (deferred: that is a schema change; this slice is pure removal, so the column + CRUD field stay vestigial pending a migration pass).
decisions.consequences: One unified DB, header-scoped per request, no marker/meta/activation/snapshot apparatus; the broken engagement-switch is gone. Build finding load-bearing for tests: the TestClient portal thread does not inherit the test thread's scope ContextVar, so real-backend test clients must send X-Engagement. Deferred follow-ons: full MCP per-engagement selection (prod MCP sends no header -> unscoped, fine for the single-engagement dogfood since prod enforcement is off), dropping the vestigial engagement_export_dir column, and repointing scripts/enumerate_commits off the now-absent db-export commits.json.
decisions.executive_summary: Records PI-beta's build: one unified DB with per-request X-Engagement resolution (marker, meta DB, activation-worker, and per-engagement-file routing deleted; engagement_scoping_enabled defaults True); switching is a client-side context change that dissolves the 'Switching failed at step 3' bug. The JSON-snapshot/db-export machinery (exporter, catalog exporter, export-dir gate, force_export, Settings.export_dir, the db-export/ tree, the file-watch RefreshService) is removed; the DB is the source of truth, and the git trail is close-out payloads + deposit-event logs. Build finding: the TestClient runs the app in a portal thread that does not inherit the test scope ContextVar, so real-backend test clients must send X-Engagement.
